nodes.label: iPhone 16 Pro DeviceModel
nodes.summary: DeviceModel entry for iPhone 16 Pro. Shares the same rasterization geometry as iPhone 17 Pro (450×920 frame, 402×874 screen). Has 4 color variants: Natural Titanium, Black Titanium, White Titanium, and Desert Titanium (Gold). The gold variant uses the imageName 'iPhone 16 Pro - Gold Titanium' and swatch hex #C9A77F. Appears third in the DeviceModel.all list, after iPhone 17 Pro.

nodes.label: iPhone 17 Pro DeviceModel
nodes.summary: DeviceModel entry for iPhone 17 Pro. Shares the same rasterization geometry as iPhone 16 Pro (450×920 frame, 402×874 screen). Has 3 color variants: Cosmic Orange, Deep Blue, and Silver. The cosmic orange variant uses swatch hex #E96A2C; silver uses #C9CCD0. Appears first in the DeviceModel.all list (after none and generic).

nodes.label: iPhone 16 Pro / 17 Pro Shared Frame Geometry
nodes.summary: Both iPhone 16 Pro and 17 Pro frame assets share identical rasterization: 450×920px PNG frame dimensions, with a 402×874px screen cutout positioned at (24, 23) in PNG coordinates, and a screen corner radius of 60px (normalized 60/450). This shared geometry allows the same compositing math to work for both models.

nodes.label: Device Frame Compositing Pipeline
nodes.summary: The device frame PNGs with transparent screens are used as overlays in Maya's rendering pipeline. The user's screen recording video is composited behind the transparent screen cutout. The screen rect (stored as normalized coordinates in screenRectNormalized) maps the video to the exact screen area. A corner radius mask (screenCornerRadiusNormalized) clips the video corners to match the device bezel curves. This compositing happens both in the preview (CanvasView) and in the export pipeline (DeviceFrameCompositor).

hyperedges.label: iPhone Pro Device Models (15/16/17 Pro + Generic + None + MacBook Pro)
